papers.title: MolRGen: Training and Evaluation Setting for De Novo Molecular Generation with Reasoning Models
papers.abstract_summary: Benchmark dataset for training reasoning-based LLMs on de novo molecular generation without ground-truth labels. Trained 24B parameter LLM using reinforcement learning.

papers.title: ATMOS: Atomic Trajectory Modeling with State Space Models for Biomolecular Dynamics
papers.abstract_summary: Generates atom-level MD trajectories for protein-ligand systems using Pairformer-based mechanisms. Trained on PDB structures and mdCATH/MISATO datasets.

papers.title: A Multi-task Large Reasoning Model for Molecular Science
papers.abstract_summary: Multi-specialist architecture with chain-of-thought reasoning enhanced by RL; achieves 50.3% improvement over baseline. CNS drug candidate design case study.

papers.title: Logos: An Evolvable Reasoning Engine for Rational Molecular Design
papers.abstract_summary: Compact molecular reasoning model integrating multi-step logical reasoning with strict chemical consistency. Matches or surpasses substantially larger general-purpose LLMs with a fraction of parameters.

papers.title: FuseDiff: Symmetry-Preserving Joint Diffusion for Dual-Target Structure-Based Drug Design
papers.abstract_summary: End-to-end diffusion model generating ligands with two pocket-specific binding poses simultaneously. Implements Dual-target Local Context Fusion mechanism.

papers.title: MMAI Gym for Science: Training Liquid Foundation Models for Drug Discovery
papers.abstract_summary: Purpose-trained Liquid Foundation Model outperforming larger general-purpose models on molecular benchmarks. Covers molecular optimization, ADMET prediction, retrosynthesis, drug-target activity.

papers.title: Development and Large-Scale Benchmarks of a Protein-Ligand Absolute Binding Free Energy Toolkit
papers.abstract_summary: Felis toolkit for high-throughput ABFE calculations. 43 protein targets and 859 ligands benchmark. Zero-shot predictions without custom force-field modifications.

papers.title: Latent Style-based Quantum Wasserstein GAN for Drug Design
papers.abstract_summary: Quantum computing + GANs for de novo drug design. Validated on IBM Heron 156-qubit quantum computer.

papers.title: Nexerra-R1: A Chemical Language Model for Reticular Materials Design
institutions: UC Berkeley
Cambridge
papers.abstract_summary: Chemical language model for MOF reticular chemistry inverse design. Supports unconstrained organic linker generation and symmetric polydentate motif design. Validates rediscovery of known MOFs and proposes CU-525.

papers.title: LaPro-DTA: Latent Dual-View Drug Representations for Generalizable Drug-Target Affinity Prediction
papers.abstract_summary: Cold-start drug-target affinity prediction with latent dual-view drug representation and stochastic perturbation. 8% MSE reduction on Davis dataset in unseen-drug settings.

papers.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery
papers.abstract_summary: Dual-layer AI agent architecture combining governance with flexibility for drug discovery. Role-based tool isolation and strategic human checkpoints for long-horizon pharmaceutical pipelines.

papers.title: DESRO: Deciphering Scientific Reasoning Steps from Outcome Data
papers.abstract_summary: DESRO bridges gap between abundant molecular experimental outcomes and scarce reasoning steps. Applied to molecule optimization with 2.3M molecular property records; highest success rate on 15/18 tasks.

papers.title: AlphaDiffract: Deep Learning for Powder X-ray Diffraction Analysis
papers.abstract_summary: Deep learning system for analyzing powder X-ray diffraction data. Predicts crystal system, space group, and lattice parameters. ConvNeXt architecture trained on 31M+ simulated patterns.

papers.title: Autotuning T-PaiNN: Transfer Learning for Graph Neural Network Interatomic Potentials
papers.abstract_summary: Transfer learning framework leveraging classical force field data to improve GNN interatomic potentials. 25x error reduction in low-data regimes for QM9. Order-of-magnitude MAE reduction.

papers.title: Garnet: Training a Neural Network Force Field for Proteins
papers.abstract_summary: GNN assigning force field parameters using continuous atom typing. Trained on QM and experimental data without relying on existing parameters. Comparable to conventional force fields.

papers.title: High-Throughput-Screening ML Workflow for Battery Materials
papers.abstract_summary: ML workflow for predicting volume changes during ion intercalation in battery materials. Screened ~1.175M transition-metal compounds using bond-length predictions.

papers.title: On the Reliability of AI Methods in Drug Discovery: Evaluation of Boltz-2
papers.abstract_summary: Extensive evaluation of Boltz-2 biomolecular foundation model on 16,780 (3CLPro) and 21,702 (TNKS2) compounds. Shows only weak-to-moderate correlations in energetic evaluations. Highlights necessity of physics-based refinement.

papers.title: ChemFlow: Hierarchical Neural Network for Chemical Mixture Properties
papers.abstract_summary: Hierarchical NN framework for chemical mixtures integrating atomic, functional group, and molecular-level features with bidirectional attention for composition-dependent property modeling.

papers.title: Thermodynamic Descriptors from MD for Out-of-Distribution Property Prediction
papers.abstract_summary: Physics-informed framework replacing structural inputs with thermodynamic properties from MD, enabling accurate property prediction for entire chemical classes absent from training set.

papers.title: Order Matters in Retrosynthesis: Structure-aware Generation via Reaction-Center-Guided Discrete Flow Matching
papers.abstract_summary: Structure-aware template-free retrosynthesis via discrete flow matching. Reduces generation steps from 500 to 20-50. 61.2% top-1 on USPTO-50k. 280K parameter model matches 65M parameter baseline with proper atom ordering.

papers.title: El Agente Sólido: A New Age(nt) for Solid State Simulations
institutions: University of Toronto
papers.abstract_summary: Multi-agent framework for automating solid-state quantum chemistry workflows. Integrates DFT, phonon calculations, and ML potentials using Quantum ESPRESSO.

papers.title: GraphAgents: Knowledge Graph-Guided Agentic AI for Cross-Domain Materials Design
institutions: MIT
papers.abstract_summary: Multi-agent framework using knowledge graphs for PFAS-alternative sustainable materials discovery. Graph traversal strategies for cross-domain materials design.

papers.title: Engineering low-symmetry colloidal crystals with optical anisotropies
institutions: Departamento de Teoría de la Señal y Comunicaciones y Sistemas Telemáticos y Computación, Universidad Rey Juan Carlos, 28942 Fuenlabrada, Spain.
Biomedical Research Networking Center in Bioengineering, Biomaterials, and Nanomedicine (CIBER-BBN), 20014 Donostia-San Sebastian, Spain.
Ikerbasque, Basque Foundation for Science, 43009 Bilbao, Spain.
papers.abstract_summary: Engineered low-symmetry colloidal crystals are emerging as promising performance-enhancing alternatives to natural materials for optical devices. However, current synthesis methods cannot precisely control structural features such as the orientation of the optical axes in these crystals. Here, DNA-m

papers.title: Harnessing semen-derived exosomes for noninvasive fundus drug delivery: A paradigm for exosome-based ocular fundus therapeutics
institutions: School of Traditional Chinese Materia Medica, Shenyang Pharmaceutical University, Shenyang 110016, Liaoning, China.
Department of Pharmaceutics, School of Pharmacy, Shenyang Pharmaceutical University, Shenyang 110016, Liaoning, China.
papers.abstract_summary: Exosomes, despite their promise as drug carriers for crossing biological barriers, remain underexplored for noninvasive posterior ocular delivery. Here, we demonstrate that semen-derived exosomes (SEVs) penetrate ocular barriers effectively, owing to their epidermal growth factor expression, which m

papers.title: Primary human intestinal organoids model enteric infection of monkeypox virus and enable scalable drug discovery
institutions: State Key Laboratory of Oral Diseases & National Center for Stomatology & National Clinical Research Center for Oral Diseases, Department of Implantology, West China Hospital of Stomatology, Sichuan University, Chengdu, Sichuan 610041, China.
University of Grenoble Alpes, CEA, Inserm, IRIG, UA13 BGE, Biomics, Grenoble, France.
Computational Bioscience Research Center, King Abdullah University of Science and Technology, Thuwal, Saudi Arabia.
papers.abstract_summary: Monkeypox virus (MPXV) infection-associated intestinal manifestations, including diarrhea and proctitis, have been frequently reported during mpox outbreaks. Here, we present clinical evidence that MPXV can directly infect the human intestine and induce lesions. Intriguingly, primary organoids cultu

institutions: Institute of Technical Thermodynamics, RWTH Aachen University, Schinkelstr. 8, 52062 Aachen, Germany
